entries.openAiUserPrompt:
You are helping with a university knowledge-graph assignment using PyReason.

PyReason edge facts look like: predicate(EntityA,EntityB):[1,1]
Use YAGO3-10 style identifiers: underscores, no spaces (e.g. Tom_Cruise, United_States).

RULE NAME: citizenFromBirthCity
LOGIC RULE (head inferred from body): citizenOf(x,y) : [1,1] <-1 bornIn(x,z) : [1,1], cityIn(z,y) : [1,1]
INTENT: If a person is born in a city and the city is in a country, infer citizenship for that country.

KNOWN NODES IN THE GRAPH (you may ONLY use these as endpoints for NEW facts, unless you introduce a new person that connects to existing city/company nodes already listed):
England, English_language, Google, Hartlepool, London, Madrid, Spain, Spanish_language, United_Kingdom, United_States

EXISTING KG FACTS (do not duplicate the same predicate+pair; you may add missing body literals such as bornIn, livesIn, worksAt):
cityIn(Hartlepool,England)
cityIn(London,United_Kingdom)
cityIn(Madrid,Spain)
companyHeadquarteredIn(Google,United_States)
officialLanguage(England,English_language)
officialLanguage(Spain,Spanish_language)

TASK:
- Propose up to 5 plausible REAL-WORLD style suggestions for this single rule (short English sentences in naturalLanguageExamples).
- Propose up to 5 NEW PyReason edge fact(s) in pyreasonFacts using ONLY: bornIn, livesIn, worksAt (when they help ground **this** rule or the citizenship chain).
- Every subject/object in pyreasonFacts must appear in the KNOWN NODES list, except you may use a NEW person name as the FIRST argument of bornIn/livesIn/worksAt if the second argument is already a known node (use underscores).
- Each pyreasonFacts item’s fact_text must be **only** the fact string ending with ``:[1,1]`` — never paste extra characters after the closing ``]`` (no ``}``, ``]}``, or stray quotes from the JSON structure).

Return ONLY one valid JSON object (straight " double quotes only, no trailing commas; pyreasonFacts may be []):
{"ruleName":"citizenFromBirthCity","naturalLanguageExamples":["..."],"pyreasonFacts":[{"fact_text":"bornIn(Some_Person,Some_City):[1,1]","name":"llmFactRuleUnique","static":true}]}
entries.openAiAssistantReply: {"ruleName":"citizenFromBirthCity","naturalLanguageExamples":["A person born in Hartlepool is a citizen of England.","If someone is born in London, they are a citizen of the United Kingdom.","A child born in Madrid will be a citizen of Spain.","An individual born in a city that is in a country is a citizen of that country.","People born in cities are often considered citizens of the country those cities are located in."],"pyreasonFacts":[{"fact_text":"bornIn(John_Smith,Hartlepool):[1,1]"},{"fact_text":"bornIn(Maria_Garcia,Madrid):[1,1]"},{"fact_text":"bornIn(Alice_Johnson,London):[1,1]"},{"fact_text":"bornIn(Bob_Brown,Hartlepool):[1,1]"},{"fact_text":"bornIn(Carla_Sanchez,Madrid):[1,1]"}]}

naturalLanguageExamples: A person born in Hartlepool is a citizen of England.
If someone is born in London, they are a citizen of the United Kingdom.
A child born in Madrid will be a citizen of Spain.
An individual born in a city that is in a country is a citizen of that country.
People born in cities are often considered citizens of the country those cities are located in.

entries.openAiAssistantReply: {"ruleName":"residenceFromCity","naturalLanguageExamples":["If John lives in Hartlepool, he lives in England.","If Maria lives in Madrid, she lives in Spain.","If Sarah lives in London, she lives in the United Kingdom.","If a person lives in a city, they live in the country where that city is located.","If someone resides in a city, they are also a resident of the corresponding country."],"pyreasonFacts":[{"fact_text":"livesIn(John_Harper,Hartlepool):[1,1]"},{"fact_text":"livesIn(Maria_Gonzalez,Madrid):[1,1]"},{"fact_text":"livesIn(Sarah_Johnson,London):[1,1]"},{"fact_text":"bornIn(John_Harper,England):[1,1]"},{"fact_text":"bornIn(Maria_Gonzalez,Spain):[1,1]"}]}

If Maria lives in Madrid, she lives in Spain.
If Sarah lives in London, she lives in the United Kingdom.
If a person lives in a city, they live in the country where that city is located.
If someone resides in a city, they are also a resident of the corresponding country.

entries.openAiAssistantReply: {"ruleName":"workCountryFromHeadquarters","naturalLanguageExamples":["If John works at Google, he is employed in the United States.","Maria works at a company headquartered in Spain, so she works in Spain.","If a person is employed at Google, they work in the United States.","David works at a company based in England, thus he works in England.","If Sarah is employed by a firm headquartered in London, she works in the United Kingdom."],"pyreasonFacts":[{"fact_text":"worksAt(John_Smith,Google):[1,1]"},{"fact_text":"worksAt(Maria_Garcia,Google):[1,1]"},{"fact_text":"worksAt(David_Brown,Google):[1,1]"},{"fact_text":"worksAt(Sarah_Johnson,Google):[1,1]"},{"fact_text":"livesIn(John_Smith,United_States):[1,1]"}]}

You may use an empty pyreasonFacts array if the examples suffice, or only add bornIn / livesIn / worksAt when useful.

companyHeadquarteredIn(Google,United_States)
